elections.note: PLATEAU metric (final election-night report, NOT the 8 p.m. first tranche). OCROV posted sequential 'Cumulative Totals' runs every ~20-40 min through election night: Run 01 (Run Date/Time 11/06/2012 07:45:25 pm) = 385,929 ballots / 0 of 1,977 precincts = vote-by-mail-only first tranche; the count then climbed and PLATEAUED at Run 09 (Run Date/Time 11/07/2012 12:47:06 am) = 862,544 ballots with ALL 1,977 of 1,977 precincts reporting (+1,735 over Run 08's 860,809 at 12:25 am = flat). Run 09 was the LAST election-night run before a ~15.5-hour gap to Run 10 (11/07/2012 04:26:39 pm = 880,893), where the multi-day canvass resumed. So 862,544 held steady through the morning of 11/07 = the final election-night report. 862,544 / 1,133,204 certified = 76.12%. (v2 had erroneously taken the 8 p.m. first tranche 385,929 = 34.06%.) Denominator confirmed: OCROV Run 23 (11/21/2012) = 1,133,204 exactly = CA SoS Statement of Vote, Voter Participation Statistics by County (Orange Total Voters 1,133,204 = Precinct 552,018 + VBM 581,186). Pre-epollbook (OC adopted iPad e-pollbooks + Voter's Choice Act vote centers at the March 2020 primary). ASV never adopted. Snapshot used: ocvote.gov live Run09 cumulative.pdf, internal Run Date/Time 11/07/2012 12:47:06 am.

PLATEAU metric (final election-night report, NOT the 8 p.m. first tranche). Run 01 (Run Date/Time 11/04/2014 07:54:23 pm) = 250,187 ballots / 0 of 1,863 precincts = vote-by-mail-only first tranche; the count climbed and PLATEAUED at Run 09 (Run Date/Time 11/05/2014 01:55:59 am) = 464,313 ballots with ALL 1,863 of 1,863 precincts reporting (+1,283 over Run 08's 463,030 at 12:26 am = flat). Run 09 was the LAST election-night run before a ~14.5-hour gap to Run 10 (11/05/2014 04:34:54 pm = 479,078), where the multi-day canvass resumed. So 464,313 held steady through the morning of 11/05 = the final election-night report. 464,313 / 640,358 certified = 72.51%. (v2 had erroneously taken the 8 p.m. first tranche 250,187 = 39.07%.) High share reflects the very low-turnout 2014 midterm (640,358 total). Denominator confirmed: OCROV Run 20 (11/17/2014) = 640,358 exactly = CA SoS Voter Participation Statistics by County (Orange Total Voters 640,358 = Precinct 252,472 + VBM 387,886). Pre-epollbook. ASV never adopted. Snapshot used: ocvote.gov live 'Run 09' cumulative.pdf, internal Run Date/Time 11/05/2014 01:55:59 am.

PLATEAU metric (final election-night report, NOT the 8 p.m. first tranche). Run 01 (Run Date/Time 11/08/2016 08:03:32 pm) = 430,263 ballots / 0 of 1,668 precincts = vote-by-mail-only first tranche; the count climbed and PLATEAUED at Run 11 (Run Date/Time 11/09/2016 01:55:57 am) = 825,317 ballots with ALL 1,668 of 1,668 precincts reporting (+1,762 over Run 10's 823,555 at 01:39 am = flat). Run 11 was the LAST election-night run before a ~15-hour gap to Run 12 (11/09/2016 04:54:55 pm = 826,582), where the multi-day canvass resumed. So 825,317 held steady through the morning of 11/09 = the final election-night report. 825,317 / 1,239,405 certified = 66.59%. (v2 had erroneously taken the 8 p.m. first tranche 430,263 = 34.72%.) Denominator confirmed: OCROV Run 30 (12/05/2016) = 1,239,405 exactly = CA SoS Voter Participation Statistics by County (Orange Total Voters 1,239,405 = Precinct 516,801 + VBM 722,604). Pre-epollbook. ASV never adopted. Snapshot used: ocvote.gov live 'Run 11' cumulative.pdf, internal Run Date/Time 11/09/2016 01:55:57 am.

PLATEAU metric (final election-night report, NOT the 8 p.m. first tranche). Run 01 (Run Date/Time 11/06/2018 07:49:18 pm) = 362,079 ballots / 0 of 1,546 precincts = vote-by-mail-only first tranche; the count climbed and PLATEAUED at Run 11 (Run Date/Time 11/07/2018 01:48:27 am) = 650,671 ballots with ALL 1,546 of 1,546 precincts reporting (+517 over Run 10's 650,154 at 01:29 am = flat). Run 11 was the LAST election-night run before a ~14.75-hour gap to Run 12 (11/07/2018 04:32:47 pm = 664,915), where the multi-day canvass resumed. So 650,671 held steady through the morning of 11/07 = the final election-night report. 650,671 / 1,106,729 certified = 58.79% (matches the San Francisco ~59% 2018 calibration). (v2 had erroneously taken the 8 p.m. first tranche 362,079 = 32.72%.) Denominator confirmed: OCROV Run 31 (11/30/2018) = 1,106,729 exactly = CA SoS Voter Participation Statistics by County (Orange Total Voters 1,106,729 = Precinct 393,423 + VBM 713,306). Pre-epollbook. ASV never adopted. Snapshot used: ocvote.gov live Run_11 cumulative.pdf, internal Run Date/Time 11/07/2018 01:48:27 am.

CA SoS per-county reporting-status page ('U'-coded row), Orange County: 100.0% precincts reporting, Ballots Cast 325,774, Last Report stamp 'Jun 7 11:42 p.m.' (First Report 'Jun 7 8:24 p.m.') at Wayback capture 20220608094259 (2022-06-08 09:42:59 UTC). FROZEN: the identical Ballots Cast figure 325,774 recurs across 2 captures, 2022-06-08 09:42:59 UTC through 2022-06-08 23:01:18 UTC, before the next available capture (2022-06-09 01:58:33 UTC) jumps to 348,130 (Last Report 'Jun 8 5:08 p.m.'), marking the canvass resuming. NOT the pre-plateau first-tranche row (page's own early-evening captures, <99% precincts reporting, were excluded per RUNBOOK 1 and the grading discipline in scratchpad sos-status-sweep.md). Certified final 636,497 Total Voters (CA SoS Statement of Vote, Voter Participation Statistics by County, https://elections.cdn.sos.ca.gov/sov/2022-primary/sov/03-voter-participation-stats-by-county.pdf). Arithmetic: 325,774/636,497 = 51.18%. vs_epollbook=post, vs_asv=n/a (per county_tech/adoption record: epollbook 2020, asv None). Grade CONFIRMED per RUNBOOK 8. Sourced from the 2026-07 CA SoS status-page sweep across the 19-county panel (scratchpad/sos-status-sweep.md, 2022-06-07 section); the cited capture and freeze/jump evidence above were independently re-derived here by matching this exact Ballots Cast figure against the raw per-capture status-page timeline (script scratchpad/build_cell_details.py), not merely transcribed from the sweep's prose summary.

PLATEAU metric (final election-night report, NOT the 8 p.m. first tranche). Vote-center era (precincts read 100% from Run 02 on, so the plateau is identified by the election-night/next-day TIME GAP, not precinct %). Run 01 (Run Time 8:00 PM, 11/08/2022) = 442,334 ballots = pre-Election-Day mail first tranche; the count climbed and PLATEAUED at Run 08 (Run Time 12:04 AM, 11/09/2022) = 611,060 ballots (+481 over Run 07's 610,579 at 11:48 pm = flat). Run 08 was the LAST election-night run before a ~16.7-hour gap to Run 09 (11/09/2022 4:44 PM = 628,975), where the multi-day canvass resumed. So 611,060 held steady through the morning of 11/09 = the final election-night report. 611,060 / 994,277 certified = 61.46%. (v2 had erroneously taken the 8 p.m. first tranche 442,334 = 44.49%.) Denominator from CA SoS Voter Participation Statistics by County (Orange Total Voters 994,277 = Precinct 164,115 + VBM 830,162); OCROV's last live run (Run 26, 12/01/2022) read 994,227, 50 short of the SoS-certified 994,277 (late-cure reconciliation) — SoS figure used as authoritative denominator. Post-epollbook (e-pollbooks + Voter's Choice Act vote centers since March 2020; the all-mail VCA model independently front-loads ballots into the pre-Election-Day mail batch). ASV never adopted. Snapshot used: ocvote.gov live Run_08 cumulative.pdf, internal Run Time 12:04 AM 11/09/2022.

CA SoS per-county reporting-status page ('U'-coded row), Orange County: 100.0% precincts reporting, Ballots Cast 400,430, Last Report stamp 'Mar 6 12:06 a.m.' (First Report 'Mar 5 8:28 p.m.') at Wayback capture 20240306144409 (2024-03-06 14:44:09 UTC). FROZEN: the identical Ballots Cast figure 400,430 recurs across 2 captures, 2024-03-06 14:44:09 UTC through 2024-03-06 18:05:49 UTC, before the next available capture (2024-03-07 03:52:36 UTC) jumps to 427,131 (Last Report 'Mar 6 5:10 p.m.'), marking the canvass resuming. NOT the pre-plateau first-tranche row (page's own early-evening captures, <99% precincts reporting, were excluded per RUNBOOK 1 and the grading discipline in scratchpad sos-status-sweep.md). Certified final 685,038 Total Voters (CA SoS Statement of Vote, Voter Participation Statistics by County, https://elections.cdn.sos.ca.gov/sov/2024-primary/sov/03-voter-participation-stats-by-county.pdf). Arithmetic: 400,430/685,038 = 58.45%. vs_epollbook=post, vs_asv=n/a (per county_tech/adoption record: epollbook 2020, asv None). Grade CONFIRMED per RUNBOOK 8. Sourced from the 2026-07 CA SoS status-page sweep across the 19-county panel (scratchpad/sos-status-sweep.md, 2024-03-05 section); the cited capture and freeze/jump evidence above were independently re-derived here by matching this exact Ballots Cast figure against the raw per-capture status-page timeline (script scratchpad/build_cell_details.py), not merely transcribed from the sweep's prose summary.

PLATEAU metric (final election-night report, NOT the 8 p.m. first tranche). Vote-center era (precincts read 100% from Run 02 on, so the plateau is identified by the election-night/next-day TIME GAP, not precinct %). Run 01 (Run Time 8:00 PM, 11/05/2024) = 718,019 ballots = pre-Election-Day mail first tranche; the count climbed through the night and the LAST election-night run was Run 09 (Run Time 12:48 AM, 11/06/2024) = 1,007,150 ballots, after which a ~16-hour gap to Run 10 (11/06/2024 4:49 PM = 1,052,969) marks the multi-day canvass resuming. So 1,007,150 held steady through the morning of 11/06 = the final election-night report. 1,007,150 / 1,417,397 certified = 71.06%. (v2 had erroneously taken the 8 p.m. first tranche 718,019 = 50.66%.) Denominator confirmed: OCROV Run 30 (12/03/2024) = 1,417,397 exactly = CA SoS Voter Participation Statistics by County (Orange Total Voters 1,417,397 = Precinct 323,223 + VBM 1,094,174). Post-epollbook (e-pollbooks + Voter's Choice Act vote centers since March 2020). ASV never adopted (OC verifies signatures by hand). Snapshot used: ocvote.gov live Run_09 cumulative.pdf, internal Run Time 12:48 AM 11/06/2024.

notes: v3 = PLATEAU correction of v2. METRIC: election-night % = (OCROV's FINAL election-night cumulative report, i.e. the count that plateaus late on election night / just past midnight and HOLDS steady through the next morning before the multi-day canvass resumes) divided by CA SoS certified final ballots (Statement of Vote, Voter Participation Statistics by County, 'Total Voters'). This deliberately rejects v2's numerator, which had taken Run 01 (the ~8 p.m. vote-by-mail-only first tranche, 0 precincts counted) and thereby undercounted by roughly 2x. METHOD: OCROV publishes sequential numbered 'Run' PDFs (cumulative.pdf, each stamped with an internal Run Date/Time) and the live ocvote.gov server STILL hosts the full historical run series for 2012-2024 (folder naming varies by year: gen2012 'RunNN', gen2014/gen2016 'Run NN', gen2018/Gen2022/GEN2024 'Run_NN'). For each general, the runs fire every ~20-40 min on election night until ALL precincts report (~12:47-1:55 a.m.), reaching a near-flat plateau, then there is a ~15-16 HOUR gap until the canvass resumes around 4:30-4:55 p.m. the next day with a large jump. The plateau = the LAST run before that gap. Plateaus (run / internal timestamp / ballots / certified / pct): 2012 Run 09 @11/07 12:47:06 am = 862,544 / 1,133,204 = 76.12%; 2014 Run 09 @11/05 01:55:59 am = 464,313 / 640,358 = 72.51%; 2016 Run 11 @11/09 01:55:57 am = 825,317 / 1,239,405 = 66.59%; 2018 Run 11 @11/07 01:48:27 am = 650,671 / 1,106,729 = 58.79%; 2022 Run 08 @11/09 12:04 am = 611,060 / 994,277 = 61.46%; 2024 Run 09 @11/06 12:48 am = 1,007,150 / 1,417,397 = 71.06%. All six numerators are PRIMARY (OCROV's own official cumulative reports). DENOMINATORS independently confirmed: OCROV's own final canvass runs hit the CA SoS certified total EXACTLY for 2012 (Run 23), 2014 (Run 20), 2016 (Run 30), 2018 (Run 31), and 2024 (Run 30); 2022's last live run (994,227) was 50 short of the SoS-certified 994,277 (late-cure reconciliation), and the SoS figure is used. 2020 GENERAL deliberately EXCLUDED as a COVID early-voting outlier; 2010 omitted (this task's scope is 2012/2014/2016/2018/2022/2024). ADOPTION: Orange County deployed iPad electronic pollbooks + Voter's Choice Act vote centers starting the March 3, 2020 primary, so 2012-2018 are PRE-epollbook and 2022/2024 are POST; automated signature verification (ASV) was NEVER adopted (staff compare envelope-image signatures by hand), so vs_asv = 'n-a' for every election. CAVEAT on interpretation: on this corrected plateau basis the pre/post-epollbook contrast is NOT a clean speed-up story (PRESIDENTIAL: 2012 pre 76.12% -> 2016 pre 66.59% -> 2024 post 71.06%; MIDTERM: 2014 pre 72.51% -> 2018 pre 58.79% -> 2022 post 61.46%). The election-night plateau share is driven mainly by turnout composition and the growth of late-arriving vote-by-mail (postmark-by-election-day ballots received and counted during the canvass), plus the VCA all-mail shift, rather than by e-pollbooks per se.
